hypothesis: If a single classifier-free guided diffusion model is trained only on metastable-state MD data and, at inference, its reverse SDE is guided by interpolated conditional scores and refined by score-aligned ascent, then it will generate configurations enriched for true transition states (committor ≈ 0.5) and recover accurate pathways across diverse systems better than linear/geodesic baselines because the difference of conditional scores approximates the reaction coordinate and SAA concentrates sampling at the barrier top while suppressing orthogonal motions.
